name: Yemen
experiences: Old City of Sana'a tower houses, Bab al-Yemen, and Souk al-Milh when local conditions permit access
Dar al-Hajar rock palace above Wadi Dhahr with a low official entry fee and a half-day 4x4 outing
Shibam's mud-brick skyscrapers and old-city lanes in Wadi Hadramawt, often paired with a Seiyun base
Aden's Crater district, Sira Fortress viewpoints, and Gold Mohur seafront on a tightly planned daylight stop
Socotra's dragon's blood tree plateau, white-sand lagoons, and 4x4-led eco-tour loops from Hadibo
avoid: Strong safety advisory: Yemen remains an active-conflict destination and most governments advise against all travel; only consider travel with a vetted fixer or operator, keep every mainland move in daylight, and expect abrupt closures, checkpoints, or cancellations.
Do not improvise independent overland travel between cities; flights, hotel transfers, 4x4 hires, and shared taxis arranged through trusted local contacts are safer than ad-hoc road moves.
ATMs, mobile data, healthcare, and card acceptance are unreliable; carry sufficient cash, re-check airport status before every flight, and leave buffer nights around any Yemenia connection.
stopoverNote: Socotra is the comparatively more realistic Yemen add-on because tours bundle 4x4s, guides, meals, and airport handling; mainland Sana'a, Shibam, and Aden only make sense with current local security guidance and generous schedule slack.

cities.name: Sana'a
cities.notes: UNESCO old city lanes, Bab al-Yemen, Souk al-Milh, and Dar al-Hajar excursions, but only with same-week security checks and hotel-arranged transport.

cities.name: Socotra Island
cities.notes: Hadibo-based eco-travel for Diksam dragon's blood trees, Qalansiyah, Detwah, Homhil and beach camps, usually run as guided 4x4 packages.

cities.name: Shibam
cities.notes: The Manhattan of the Desert: mud-brick tower blocks, skyline viewpoints, and easy pairing with Seiyun hotels and drivers.

cities.name: Aden
cities.notes: Crater and Tawahi seafront stops work best as a short daylight logistics base before or after Yemenia flights.

itinerary.sim: YOU, Sabafon, and Yemen Mobile are the names to ask for, but service quality changes sharply by governorate; expect roughly ₹500–₹1,500 for a starter data top-up and treat offline maps as essential because signal and recharges can disappear outside the main city cores.

Sana'a.name: Sana'a
Sana'a.note: Treat Sana'a as a fixer-led heritage stop, not a free-roaming city break; keep hotels inside known compounds or established Old City properties and avoid night movement.

days.theme: Bab al-Yemen, UNESCO lanes, and a careful first read of Old Sana'a with a local fixer

activities.name: Old City of Sana'a and Bab al-Yemen walking circuit
Souk al-Milh browsing and tea stop
Hotel-arranged fixer or local guide for controlled Old City access
Great Mosque of Sana'a exterior/photo stop

days.theme: Dar al-Hajar and a daylight-only look at Sana'a's broader historic setting

activities.name: Dar al-Hajar (Rock Palace) entry
Private 4x4 or trusted taxi to Wadi Dhahr and Dar al-Hajar
Al Saleh Mosque exterior stop
Return to hotel before dark

days.theme: Use the extra Sana'a day only if advisories, checkpoints, and your fixer all stay favorable

activities.name: National Museum of Yemen or alternate museum stop if open
Old City rooftop tea and photo session
Buffer time for permit checks, airport reconfirmations, or schedule slips

Shibam.name: Shibam
Shibam.note: Most travelers sleep in Seiyun and visit Shibam by arranged car; the old city itself is small, but the skyline and nearby wadis reward a full daylight block.

days.theme: Mud-skyscraper skyline, old lanes, and a heritage day in Wadi Hadramawt

activities.name: Shibam old city entry and skyline walk
Shibam Museum or local heritage room if open
Seiyun to Shibam return by trusted taxi or hotel car
Town-square tea stop with skyline views

days.theme: Add a second Hadramawt day for Seiyun context and a slower architectural loop

activities.name: Sunrise or late-afternoon Shibam exterior viewpoints
Seiyun Palace Museum exterior or paired Seiyun stop
Private driver for a wider Hadramawt half-day loop

Aden.name: Aden
Aden.note: Keep Aden functional: sleep near your airport or onward meeting point, move only in daylight, and use it as a short transition before Socotra or departure.

days.theme: Crater district viewpoints, seafront air, and logistics before the next flight

activities.name: Sira Fortress exterior and harbor viewpoints
Tawila Tanks and Crater district walk
Gold Mohur Beach stop
Registered taxi between airport, hotel, and Crater/Tawahi

Socotra Island.name: Socotra Island
Socotra Island.note: Socotra works best as an operator-run 4x4 circuit from Hadibo; most real costs are bundled into packages covering airport handling, camping, meals, and guide support.

days.theme: Hadibo arrival, airport handling, and an easy first-night island reset

activities.name: Yemenia arrival handling and Hadibo hotel transfer
Hadibo corniche or local market walk
Delisha or nearby beach sunset stop

days.theme: Diksam Plateau, Firmihin, and the dragon's blood tree landscapes that define Socotra

activities.name: Diksam Plateau and Firmihin dragon's blood tree 4x4 day tour with local guide
Dragon's Blood Trees protected area access
Wadi Dirhur picnic or canyon viewpoints

days.theme: Qalansiyah, Detwah Lagoon, and Socotra's best-known west-coast color palette

activities.name: Qalansiyah and Detwah Lagoon 4x4 day trip
Boat or beach time if sea conditions cooperate

days.theme: Use the extra island day for Homhil, Arher, or a package beach camp before flying out

activities.name: Homhil or Arher 4x4 extension with driver-guide
Beach camp or eco-camp supplement if not already bundled
Final Hadibo resupply and airport buffer

connections.method: Yemenia or another currently operating domestic connection to Seiyun is the least-friction option; overland shared taxis exist but should only be considered with same-day local security clearance.
connections.cost: ₹12,000–₹24,000 by flight / about ₹5,000–₹8,500 per shared-taxi seat

connections.method: Return to Seiyun for a domestic flight when available; otherwise use a fixer-arranged shared taxi or private 4x4 with buffer time and daylight-only movement.
connections.cost: ₹10,000–₹22,000 by flight / ₹5,000–₹8,500 per shared-taxi seat / ₹5,800–₹10,000 for private 4x4 day hire

connections.method: Yemenia domestic flight is the standard link; most travelers roll the ticket into a Socotra package and let the island operator handle airport pickup.

extras: October to March is the safest weather window for combining mainland Yemen and Socotra: mainland heat is less punishing, and Socotra sits outside its rough June-to-September monsoon pattern.

Travel advisory: Yemen remains affected by active conflict, airstrike risk, kidnappings, mines, and abrupt checkpoint controls. Re-check live government advisories before booking and again before every intercity leg.

Old City of Sana'a and Bab al-Yemen are generally public/free if accessible at all; Dar al-Hajar is one of the few mainstream heritage stops with a small paid ticket, usually about 500–1,000 YER (roughly ₹165–₹330).
Shibam's streets can be walked without a formal gate ticket, but the museum or heritage-room stop usually carries a modest 500–1,000 YER fee and is best paired with a Seiyun-based driver.

Socotra's dragon's blood tree areas usually do not sell a separate standalone ticket; the real cost is the bundled 4x4 day-tour or multi-day package that covers the driver, guide, and route permissions.

Shared taxis are common on Yemeni corridors, but for foreign travelers they are a last-resort logistics tool rather than a sightseeing experience; hotel or fixer-booked transport is the safer planning default.
